devanagari: सूर्यवंशवर्णनं वैवस्वतमनोः पुत्रस्य स्त्रीभावापत्तिश्च । श्रीराजोवाच । मन्वन्तराणि सर्वाणि त्वयोक्तानि श्रुतानि मे । वीर्याणि अनन्तवीर्यस...
english_devnagari: sūryavaṃśavarṇanaṃ vaivasvatamanoḥ putrasya strībhāvāpattiścaśrīrājovācamanvantarāṇi sarvāṇi tvayoktāni śrutāni mevīryāṇi anantavīryasya harestatra kṛtāni ca
translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) requested:
All the Manu-epochs (Manvantaras) described by you and the (account of) glorious heroic feats of Lord Hari of infinite prowess and energy, achieved during those epochs as narrated by you, have been heard by me.

devanagari: योऽसौ सत्यव्रतो नाम राजर्षिः द्रविडेश्वरः । ज्ञानं योऽतीतकल्पान्ते लेभे पुरुषसेवया ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yo'sau satyavrato nāma rājarṣiḥ draviḍeśvaraḥjñānaṃ yo'tītakalpānte lebhe puruṣasevayā
translation: I learnt from you that he who, at the end of the last Kalpa, was the royal sage named Satyavrata, the King of Draviḍas (or Draviḍa country), attained spiritual knowledge by worshipping the Supreme Person, has verily become a Manu (Śrāddhadeva), the Son of Vivasvat; I have also heard from you the (history of) his sons, the kings, of whom Ikṣvāku was prominent.

devanagari: स वै विवस्वतः पुत्रो मनुः आसीद् इति श्रुतम् । त्वत्तस्तस्य सुताः प्रोक्ता इक्ष्वाकुप्रमुखा नृपाः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai vivasvataḥ putro manuḥ āsīd iti śrutamtvattastasya sutāḥ proktā ikṣvākupramukhā nṛpāḥ
translation: I learnt from you that he who, at the end of the last Kalpa, was the royal sage named Satyavrata, the King of Draviḍas (or Draviḍa country), attained spiritual knowledge by worshipping the Supreme Person, has verily become a Manu (Śrāddhadeva), the Son of Vivasvat; I have also heard from you the (history of) his sons, the kings, of whom Ikṣvāku was prominent.

devanagari: तेषां वंशं पृथग्ब्रह्मन् वंशानुचरितानि च । कीर्तयस्व महाभाग नित्यं शुश्रूषतां हि नः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ vaṃśaṃ pṛthagbrahman vaṃśānucaritāni cakīrtayasva mahābhāga nityaṃ śuśrūṣatāṃ hi naḥ
translation: Be pleased to describe to us in details, Oh Brahman, the history of the race and the deeds of those belonging to that line, separately, as we are ever eager to listen to them, Oh highly blessed sage.

devanagari: ये भूता ये भविष्याश्च भवन्ति अद्यतनाश्च ये । तेषां नः पुण्यकीर्तीनां सर्वेषां वद विक्रमान् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ye bhūtā ye bhaviṣyāśca bhavanti adyatanāśca yeteṣāṃ naḥ puṇyakīrtīnāṃ sarveṣāṃ vada vikramān
translation: Kindly relate unto us the heroic exploits of all those kings of hallowed (or sanctifying) renown who belonged to the past, who will rule in future and who belong to the present age.

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । एवं परीक्षिता राज्ञा सदसि ब्रह्मवादिनाम् । पृष्टः प्रोवाच भगवान् शुकः परमधर्मवित् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīsūta uvācaevaṃ parīkṣitā rājñā sadasi brahmavādināmpṛṣṭaḥ provāca bhagavān śukaḥ paramadharmavit
translation:
Sūta said:
When accosted in these words by king Parīkṣit, in the assembly of the exponents of Vedas and Brahman, venerable Śuka, the expert in the highest form dharma[1] proceeded to narrate in details.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
This dharma is ‘the extolling the excellences of the Lord’ according to Bhāgavata Candrikā, while ‘Protection of the subjects is the dharma of crowned kings’ (Padaratnāvalī).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । श्रूयतां मानवो वंशः प्राचुर्येण परंतप । न शक्यते विस्तरतो वक्तुं वर्षशतैरपि ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaśrūyatāṃ mānavo vaṃśaḥ prācuryeṇa paraṃtapana śakyate vistarato vaktuṃ varṣaśatairapi
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Oh chastiser of enemies! Listen to the main events in the history of the race of Manu (in brief), for it will not be possible to describe it in details for centuries together.

devanagari: परावरेषां भूतानां आत्मा यः पुरुषः परः । स एवासीद् इदं विश्वं कल्पान्ते अन्यत् न किञ्चन ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: parāvareṣāṃ bhūtānāṃ ātmā yaḥ puruṣaḥ paraḥsa evāsīd idaṃ viśvaṃ kalpānte anyat na kiñcana
translation: At the end of the Kalpa, there was only the Supreme Person, who is the Soul of all beings high and low. There was neither universe nor anything else.

devanagari: तस्य नाभेः समभवत् पद्मकोषो हिरण्मयः । तस्मिन् जज्ञे महाराज स्वयंभूः चतुराननः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya nābheḥ samabhavat padmakoṣo hiraṇmayaḥtasmin jajñe mahārāja svayaṃbhūḥ caturānanaḥ
translation: From his navel sprouted forth the calyx of a gold lotus wherein was born the self-created four-faced god Brahmā, Oh mighty king.

devanagari: मरीचिः मनसस्तस्य जज्ञे तस्यापि कश्यपः । दाक्षायण्यां ततोऽदित्यां विवस्वान् अभवत् सुतः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: marīciḥ manasastasya jajñe tasyāpi kaśyapaḥdākṣāyaṇyāṃ tato'dityāṃ vivasvān abhavat sutaḥ
translation: From his mind was born Marīci and from Marīci was born Kaśyapa. From Kaśyapa and his wife Aditi, the daughter of Dakṣa (Prajāpati) was born a son called Vivasvat.

devanagari: ततो मनुः श्राद्धदेवः संज्ञायामास भारत । श्रद्धायां जनयामास दश पुत्रान् स आत्मवान् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tato manuḥ śrāddhadevaḥ saṃjñāyāmāsa bhārataśraddhāyāṃ janayāmāsa daśa putrān sa ātmavān
translation: From Vivasvat and his wife Saṃjñā was born Śrāddha-deva, the Manu and through his wife Śraddhā, the self-controlled eminent Śrāddhadeva begot the (following) ten sons:

devanagari: इक्ष्वाकुनृगशर्याति दिष्टधृष्ट करूषकान् । नरिष्यन्तं पृषध्रं च नभगं च कविं विभुः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: ikṣvākunṛgaśaryāti diṣṭadhṛṣṭa karūṣakānnariṣyantaṃ pṛṣadhraṃ ca nabhagaṃ ca kaviṃ vibhuḥ
translation: (Namely) Ikṣvāku, Nṛga, Śaryāti, Diṣṭa, Dhṛṣta, Karūṣaka, Narīṣyat, Pṛṣaddhra, Nabhaga, Kavi.

devanagari: अप्रजस्य मनोः पूर्वं वसिष्ठो भगवान् किल । मित्रावरुणयोः इष्टिं प्रजार्थं अकरोद् विभुः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: aprajasya manoḥ pūrvaṃ vasiṣṭho bhagavān kilamitrāvaruṇayoḥ iṣṭiṃ prajārthaṃ akarod vibhuḥ
translation: It is reported that in the beginning, when Manu was childless, the worshipful and (spiritually) powerful Vasiṣṭha performed a sacrifice, in honour of Mitra and Varuṇa.

devanagari: तत्र श्रद्धा मनोः पत्‍नी होतारं समयाचत । दुहित्रर्थं उपागम्य प्रणिपत्य पयोव्रता ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra śraddhā manoḥ pat‍nī hotāraṃ samayācataduhitrarthaṃ upāgamya praṇipatya payovratā
translation: In that sacrifice, Śraddhā, the wife of Manu who was observing the payo-vrata vow, approached the Hotṛ-priest, paid obeisance to him and begged for the birth of a daughter.

devanagari: प्रेषितोऽध्वर्युणा होता ध्यायन् तत् सुसमाहितः । हविषि व्यचरत् तेन वषट्कारं गृणन् द्विजः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: preṣito'dhvaryuṇā hotā dhyāyan tat susamāhitaḥhaviṣi vyacarat tena vaṣaṭkāraṃ gṛṇan dvijaḥ
translation: The Hotṛ (the reciter of Vedic mantras in a sacrifice) who was directed by the Adhvaryu (the officiating head sacrificial priest), was brooding over the request of Śraddhā. The Brāhmaṇa (contemplating over her request) with full concentration of mind, offered oblations while uttering the mystic word—Vaṣaṭ (and propitiated the fire).

devanagari: होतुस्तद् व्यभिचारेण कन्येला नाम साभवत् । तां विलोक्य मनुः प्राह नाति हृष्टमना गुरुम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: hotustad vyabhicāreṇa kanyelā nāma sābhavattāṃ vilokya manuḥ prāha nāti hṛṣṭamanā gurum
translation: As a consequence of the dereliction of duty on the part of the Holṛ (the reciter of mantras), a daughter named Ilā was born. Not being much delighted at the sight of the daughter (when the sacrifice was performed for the birth of a son), Manu addressed the preceptor (as follows):

devanagari: भगवन् किमिदं जातं कर्म वो ब्रह्मवादिनाम् । विपर्ययं अहो कष्टं मैवं स्याद् ब्रह्मविक्रिया ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavan kimidaṃ jātaṃ karma vo brahmavādināmviparyayaṃ aho kaṣṭaṃ maivaṃ syād brahmavikriyā
translation: “Worshipful Sir, what is this that the act (of sacrificer) performed by the expert exponents of Vedas has led to the contrary result? Ah! What a pity! There should not have been such a reversal (in the fruit) of Vedic Mantras.

devanagari: यूयं मंत्रविदो युक्ताः तपसा दग्धकिल्बिषाः । कुतः संकल्पवैषम्यं अनृतं विबुधेष्विव ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: yūyaṃ maṃtravido yuktāḥ tapasā dagdhakilbiṣāḥkutaḥ saṃkalpavaiṣamyaṃ anṛtaṃ vibudheṣviva
translation: You are experts in the knowledge and sacrificial application of the Mantras and are self-disciplined. You have burnt down all your sins by your austerities. What is the cause of the failure of this expectation. This is as impossible as falsehood in the case of gods. (Just as what gods think or say turns out to be true, your will power should have similarly prevailed)”.

devanagari: निशम्य तद्वचः तस्य भगवान् प्रपितामहः । होतुर्व्यतिक्रमं ज्ञात्वा बभाषे रविनन्दनम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: niśamya tadvacaḥ tasya bhagavān prapitāmahaḥhoturvyatikramaṃ jñātvā babhāṣe ravinandanam
translation: Hearing this (complaining) speech (of Manu), the worshipful great-grandfather[2] Vasiṣṭha came to know the transgression committed by the reciter of mantras (Hotṛ) and replied to Vaivasvata Manu (the son of the Sun, Vivasvat).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
It should have been grand-father’s grand-father as can be seen from the following genealogy of Śuka from Vasiṣṭha:
Vasiṣṭha—Śakti—Parāśara—Vyāsa—Śuka but if pra-pitāmaha is to be defended (as Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa does it) by saying that the father and the son are to be regarded as one as per the Śruti text ātmaiva putranāmā'si, the chain of identity will reach to Śuka also.

devanagari: एतत् संकल्पवैषम्यं होतुस्ते व्यभिचारतः । तथापि साधयिष्ये ते सुप्रजास्त्वं स्वतेजसा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: etat saṃkalpavaiṣamyaṃ hotuste vyabhicārataḥtathāpi sādhayiṣye te suprajāstvaṃ svatejasā
translation: “This frustration of your expectation is due to the dereliction of duty on the part of the Hotṛ. Yet by virtue of my spiritual power, I shall accomplish your object of having a good son”.

devanagari: एवं व्यवसितो राजन् भगवान् स महायशाः । अस्तौषीद् आदिपुरुषं इलायाः पुंस्त्वकाम्यया ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vyavasito rājan bhagavān sa mahāyaśāḥastauṣīd ādipuruṣaṃ ilāyāḥ puṃstvakāmyayā
translation: The adorable Vasiṣṭha of great renown, determined thus and with the desire of securing manhood to Ilā prayed Lord Viṣṇu, The Eternal Person.

devanagari: तस्मै कामवरं तुष्टो भगवान् हरिरीश्वरः । ददौ इविलाभवत् तेन सुद्युम्नः पुरुषर्षभः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmai kāmavaraṃ tuṣṭo bhagavān harirīśvaraḥdadau ivilābhavat tena sudyumnaḥ puruṣarṣabhaḥ
translation: The glorious Lord Hari, The Ruler (of the universe), being pleased with Vasiṣṭha, conferred on him the boon sought for (by him). In virtue of that boon, Ilā became Sudyumna, the foremost among men.

devanagari: स एकदा महाराज विचरन् मृगयां वने । वृतः कतिपयामात्यैः अश्वं आरुह्य सैन्धवम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ekadā mahārāja vicaran mṛgayāṃ vanevṛtaḥ katipayāmātyaiḥ aśvaṃ āruhya saindhavam
translation: On one occasion, riding a horse of Sindhu breed, and accompanied by a few ministers, he went on a hunting expedition in a forest, Oh great king.

devanagari: प्रगृह्य रुचिरं चापं शरांश्च परमाद्‍भुतान् । दंशितोऽनुमृगं वीरो जगाम दिशमुत्तराम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: pragṛhya ruciraṃ cāpaṃ śarāṃśca paramād‍bhutāndaṃśito'numṛgaṃ vīro jagāma diśamuttarām
translation: Clad in an armour and arming himself with a beautiful bow and extremely wonderful arrows, the hero went to the northern direction, in pursuit of the game (a deer).

devanagari: सु कुमातो वनं मेरोः अधस्तात् प्रविवेश ह । यत्रास्ते भगवान् शर्वो रममाणः सहोमया ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: su kumāto vanaṃ meroḥ adhastāt praviveśa hayatrāste bhagavān śarvo ramamāṇaḥ sahomayā
translation: It is reported that the prince entered a forest on the foothills of mount Meru, where the glorious Lord Śiva sports amorously with his Spouse Umā.

devanagari: तस्मिन् प्रविष्ट एवासौ सुद्युम्नः परवीरहा । अपश्यत् स्त्रियमात्मानं अश्वं च वडवां नृप ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin praviṣṭa evāsau sudyumnaḥ paravīrahāapaśyat striyamātmānaṃ aśvaṃ ca vaḍavāṃ nṛpa
translation: Hardly did Sudyumna, that vanquisher of inimical warriors, enter the forest, when he found himself transformed into a woman and the horse into a mare, Oh king.

devanagari: तथा तदनुगाः सर्वे आत्मलिङ्ग विपर्ययम् । दृष्ट्वा विमनसोऽभूवन् वीक्षमाणाः परस्परम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā tadanugāḥ sarve ātmaliṅga viparyayamdṛṣṭvā vimanaso'bhūvan vīkṣamāṇāḥ parasparam
translation: Noticing a change of sex in themselves, all his followers became dejected at heart and began to stare at each other.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । कथं एवं गुणो देशः केन वा भगवन् कृतः । प्रश्नमेनं समाचक्ष्व परं कौतूहलं हि नः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācakathaṃ evaṃ guṇo deśaḥ kena vā bhagavan kṛtaḥpraśnamenaṃ samācakṣva paraṃ kautūhalaṃ hi naḥ
translation:
King Parīkṣit asked:
Oh venerable Sir! How is it that the region came to acquire such power? Who made it so? Be pleased to answer this query, as our curiosity has become intense.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एकदा गिरिशं द्रष्टुं ऋषयस्तत्र सुव्रताः । दिशो वितिमिराभासाः कुर्वन्तः समुपागमन् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaekadā giriśaṃ draṣṭuṃ ṛṣayastatra suvratāḥdiśo vitimirābhāsāḥ kurvantaḥ samupāgaman
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
On one occasion, sages, who were strict observers of sacred vows, came to that region (Ilāvṛta) to pay visit to god Śiva, the lord of mount Kailāsa, dispelling darkness from all quarters (with their spiritual lustre).

devanagari: तान् विलोक्य अंबिका देवी विवासा व्रीडिता भृशम् । भर्तुरङ्गात समुत्थाय नीवीमाश्वथ पर्यधात् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tān vilokya aṃbikā devī vivāsā vrīḍitā bhṛśambharturaṅgāta samutthāya nīvīmāśvatha paryadhāt
translation: Having seen them, goddess Pārvatī, who was then ungarmented, got extremely abashed, and quickly getting up from her consort’s lap, put on her raiments immediately.

devanagari: ऋषयोऽपि तयोर्वीक्ष्य प्रसङ्गं रममाणयोः । निवृत्ताः प्रययुस्तस्मात् नरनारायणाश्रमम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣayo'pi tayorvīkṣya prasaṅgaṃ ramamāṇayoḥnivṛttāḥ prayayustasmāt naranārāyaṇāśramam
translation: Noting the union of the divine couple in amorous enjoyment, the sages instantly turned back and proceeded to the hermitage of Nara and Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: तदिदं भगवान् आह प्रियायाः प्रियकाम्यया । स्थानं यः प्रविशेदेतत् स वै योषिद् भवेदिति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tadidaṃ bhagavān āha priyāyāḥ priyakāmyayāsthānaṃ yaḥ praviśedetat sa vai yoṣid bhavediti
translation: Then, with a desire to satisfy his divine beloved, the glorious god Śiva announced: ‘Whoever enters this region, shall (automatically and instantly) turn into a veritable woman.

devanagari: तत ऊर्ध्वं वनं तद्वै पुरुषा वर्जयन्ति हि । सा चानुचरसंयुक्ता विचचार वनाद् वनम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tata ūrdhvaṃ vanaṃ tadvai puruṣā varjayanti hisā cānucarasaṃyuktā vicacāra vanād vanam
translation: Hence all males avoid going to that forest since then. She (Sudyumna, now a woman Ilā) went on wandering from one forest to another, accompanied by her retinue.

devanagari: अथ तां आश्रमाभ्याशे चरन्तीं प्रमदोत्तमाम् । स्त्रीभिः परिवृतां वीक्ष्य चकमे भगवान् बुधः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tāṃ āśramābhyāśe carantīṃ pramadottamāmstrībhiḥ parivṛtāṃ vīkṣya cakame bhagavān budhaḥ
translation: Now beholding that excellent young woman surrounded by ladies, (leisurely) wandering on the precincts of his hermitage, god Budha (the presiding deity of the planet Mercury) conceived an amorous passion for her.

devanagari: सापि तं चकमे सुभ्रूः सोमराजसुतं पतिम् । स तस्यां जनयामास पुरूरवसमात्मजम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sāpi taṃ cakame subhrūḥ somarājasutaṃ patimsa tasyāṃ janayāmāsa purūravasamātmajam
translation: That lady with beautiful eyebrows, in her turn, reciprocated his love and sought him, the son of King Soma, as her husband. He begot on her a son named Purūravas.

devanagari: एवं स्त्रीत्वं अनुप्राप्तः सुद्युम्नो मानवो नृपः । सस्मार स कुलाचार्यं वसिष्ठमिति शुश्रुम ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ strītvaṃ anuprāptaḥ sudyumno mānavo nṛpaḥsasmāra sa kulācāryaṃ vasiṣṭhamiti śuśruma
translation: We hear it reported that King Sudyumna of Manu’s race who was thus transformed into a woman, happened to remember Vasiṣṭha, his family preceptor.

devanagari: स तस्य तां दशां दृष्ट्वा कृपया भृशपीडितः । सुद्युम्नस्याशयन् पुंस्त्वं उपाधावत शंकरम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tasya tāṃ daśāṃ dṛṣṭvā kṛpayā bhṛśapīḍitaḥsudyumnasyāśayan puṃstvaṃ upādhāvata śaṃkaram
translation: (As soon as he was thus remembered, Vasiṣṭha appeared on the scene). Seeing that sad plight of Sudyumna, Vasiṣṭha was overwhelmed with compassion. Being deeply moved, and wishing to restore the manhood of Sudyumna, he approached (appealed to) god Śaṅkara.

devanagari: तुष्टस्तस्मै स भगवान् ऋषये प्रियमावहन् । स्वां च वाचं ऋतां कुर्वन् इदमाह विशाम्पते ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tuṣṭastasmai sa bhagavān ṛṣaye priyamāvahansvāṃ ca vācaṃ ṛtāṃ kurvan idamāha viśāmpate
translation: Being pleased with Vasiṣṭha and with a view to grant his prayer as well as to retain the veracity of his own utterance, the glorious god Śiva spoke as follows, Oh King.

devanagari: मासं पुमान् स भविता मासं स्त्री तव गोत्रजः । इत्थं व्यवस्थया कामं सुद्युम्नोऽवतु मेदिनीम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: māsaṃ pumān sa bhavitā māsaṃ strī tava gotrajaḥitthaṃ vyavasthayā kāmaṃ sudyumno'vatu medinīm
translation: ‘Sudyumna who is born of your race, shall be a man for one month and a woman for another month alternately. May he protect the earth at his will, under this arrangement’[3].
purport:
Footnote [3]:
The story is briefly mentioned in Mahābhārata Ādi 75.16, Anu 147. 26, Liṅga P. 1.65.

devanagari: आचार्यानुग्रहात् कामं लब्ध्वा पुंस्त्वं व्यवस्थया । पालयामास जगतीं नाभ्यनन्दन् स्म तं प्रजाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryānugrahāt kāmaṃ labdhvā puṃstvaṃ vyavasthayāpālayāmāsa jagatīṃ nābhyanandan sma taṃ prajāḥ
translation: Having accomplished his desire of restoration to manhood through the grace of his preceptor, he ruled over the earth under the above-mentioned arrangement. But his subjects did not approve of it.

devanagari: तस्योत्कलो गयो राजन् विमलश्च सुतास्त्रयः । दक्षिणापथराजानो बभूवुः धर्मवत्सलाः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyotkalo gayo rājan vimalaśca sutāstrayaḥdakṣiṇāpatharājāno babhūvuḥ dharmavatsalāḥ
translation: He got three sons, Utkala, Gaya and Vimala. They became righteous rulers of the Deccan (the southern region including Mahārāṣṭra).

devanagari: ततः परिणते काले प्रतिष्ठानपतिः प्रभुः । पुरूरवस उत्सृज्य गां पुत्राय गतो वनम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ pariṇate kāle pratiṣṭhānapatiḥ prabhuḥpurūravasa utsṛjya gāṃ putrāya gato vanam
translation:
With the ripeness of the age, King Sudyumna, the ruler over pratiṣṭhāna[4] handed over (the kingdom of) the earth to (his son) Purūravas, and went to forest (for performance of penance).
[back to top]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Most probably Jhusi opposite to Allahabad across the Gaṅgā. Mentioned in Mahābhārata Vana. 85.76. According to Liṅga P. 1.65 the kingdom of Ikṣvākus was divided among Sudyumna’s sons, while according to Vasiṣṭha’s advice, the son of Ilā (the female transformation of Sudyumna) Purūravas, was given Pratiṣṭhānapura as he had no hereditory rights on the kingdom of Ikṣvāku. But Bhāgavata Purāṇa 9.2.2 shows that Ikṣvāku was born, when Sudyumna left for the forest.
D.P. Mishra’s conjectural identification of this Pratiṣṭhāna with ‘Ortospana’ (near Kabul—‘nearer Bactria, the home province of Purūravas’) of Greek writers deserves consideration. Vide studies—Proto. Hist. of Ind. pp. 116-17
